task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2577: Content rewrite pipeline - transform deck content for presentations ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git push origin upstream/dev:dev --force` 3. `git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2577` 4. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2577 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 5. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 6. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 7. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: Content rewrite pipeline - transform deck content for presentations (#2577)"` 8. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2577` 9. Open PR on the **fork** for CodeRabbit review (NOT on upstream): ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie \ --base dev \ --head sophie/issue-2577 \ --title "feat: Content rewrite pipeline - transform deck content for presentations" \ --body "Implements slideheroes/2025slideheroes#2577 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan. > This PR is on the fork for CodeRabbit review. It will be promoted to upstream automatically once CR is satisfied and CI passes." ``` **IMPORTANT:** - Open the PR on the FORK (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie`), NOT on upstream (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes`). - The PR will be automatically promoted to upstream after CodeRabbit review. - Do NOT open a PR on upstream directly. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Create a dedicated rewriter agent in Mastra with model routing and RAG support, following the established editor/partner agent patterns - Build prompt templates leveraging the existing `outlineRewriteInstructions` partial with audience brief interpolation for tone adaptation - Implement server actions as preview-then-apply flow, reusing `saveStoryboardContent` for persistence - Keep the rewriter as a standalone action rather than automatic workflow step, giving users explicit control over destructive content changes
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Where should the rewriter run in the workflow? **Options Considered:** 1. Add to `postProcessWorkflow` parallel fan-out alongside partner/validator/whisperer/editor 2. Sequential step after `postProcessWorkflow` merge 3. Standalone server action triggered independently **Chosen Option:** 3 **Rationale:** Standalone server action. The rewriter applies destructive changes to slide content (unlike review agents that suggest), so it should be an explicit user-triggered action rather than automatic in the workflow.
Design Choice 2: Should rewrite output go to a new table or update existing storyboard_contents? **Options Considered:** 1. Create new `rewrite_outputs` table to preserve original 2. Update `storyboard_contents` directly with rewritten slides **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Update `storyboard_contents` directly. The existing `saveStoryboardContent` service supports upserts, and storing intermediate versions adds complexity without clear value. Users can regenerate if needed.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Rewriter Agent Infrastructure Create the Mastra agent with model routing, RAG context support, and output schemas for rewritten content and speaker notes.
Task 1: Create Rewriter Agent Define the rewriter agent following the established agent pattern with structured output schemas. - Create `packages/mastra/src/agents/rewriter-agent.ts` following `editor-agent.ts` pattern - Define `RewriterSlideSchema` with fields: `slideId`, `rewrittenHeadline`, `rewrittenBody`, `speakerNotes`, `rationale` - Define `RewriterOutputSchema` as array of `RewriterSlideSchema` plus `overallSummary` - Agent instructions should emphasize: clarity, impact, presentation best practices, audience tone adaptation - Use `createDynamicModelForAgent("rewriter")` for runtime model swapping
Task 2: Add Model Routing Configuration Wire the rewriter into the model routing system for fallback chains and task-type routing. - Update `packages/mastra/src/config/model-routing.ts`: add `"rewriter"` to `AgentName` union - Add `rewriter` entry to `AGENT_MODEL_POLICY` with default `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514`, fast `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, fallbacks including `openai/gpt-4o` - Update `packages/mastra/src/resilience/resilient-agent-runner.ts`: add `case "rewriter"` to `resolveAgentRunner()` switch
Task 3: Configure RAG Retrieval Enable the rewriter to access user uploads and playbooks for context-aware rewriting. - Update `packages/mastra/src/rag/context-provider.ts`: add `"rewriter"` to `RAGAgentName` union - Add `rewriter` entry to `DEFAULT_RETRIEVAL_CONFIGS` with content types `USER_UPLOAD`, `PLAYBOOK`; `topK: 5`, `minScore: 0.12`
Task 4: Register in Agent Catalog Make the agent available to the resilient runner and discoverable in the agent registry. - Update `packages/mastra/src/agents/registry.ts`: add to `AGENT_CATALOG` array and `LAUNCH_AGENTS` object - Update `packages/mastra/src/agents/index.ts`: re-export `rewriterAgent` and schemas - Update `packages/mastra/src/mastra.ts`: add `rewriterAgent` to the `agents` object in `getMastra()`
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Create the Mastra rewriter agent and wire it into model routing, RAG retrieval, and the agent registry — following the existing `editor-agent.ts` pattern throughout. **New file: `packages/mastra/src/agents/rewriter-agent.ts`** - Mirror the structure of `editor-agent.ts` - Define `RewriterSlideSchema` (Zod) with fields: `slideId`, `rewrittenHeadline`, `rewrittenBody`, `speakerNotes`, `rationale` - Define `RewriterOutputSchema` as an array of `RewriterSlideSchema` plus an `overallSummary` field - Agent instructions must emphasize clarity, impact, presentation best practices, and audience tone adaptation - Use `createDynamicModelForAgent("rewriter")` for runtime model swapping **Model routing — `packages/mastra/src/config/model-routing.ts`** - Add `"rewriter"` to the `AgentName` union type - Add a `rewriter` entry to `AGENT_MODEL_POLICY`: default model `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514`, fast model `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, fallbacks including `openai/gpt-4o` **Resilient runner — `packages/mastra/src/resilience/resilient-agent-runner.ts`** - Add `case "rewriter"` to the `resolveAgentRunner()` switch statement **RAG context — `packages/mastra/src/rag/context-provider.ts`** - Add `"rewriter"` to the `RAGAgentName` union - Add a `rewriter` entry to `DEFAULT_RETRIEVAL_CONFIGS` with content types `USER_UPLOAD` and `PLAYBOOK`, `topK: 5`, `minScore: 0.12` **Registry and exports** - `packages/mastra/src/agents/registry.ts`: add `rewriterAgent` to `AGENT_CATALOG` array and `LAUNCH_AGENTS` object - `packages/mastra/src/agents/index.ts`: re-export `rewriterAgent` and its schemas - `packages/mastra/src/mastra.ts`: add `rewriterAgent` to the `agents` object inside `getMastra()` ```
### Phase 2: Prompt Templates Create ## Coding Rules # Coding Rules These rules document the actual code quality patterns used in the SlideHeroes codebase. ## TypeScript Patterns - Strict mode compliance: enable all strict TypeScript options - Use explicit local type aliases rather than inline complex types - Use `z.infer` for Zod-derived types - Use `instanceof Error` narrowing in catch blocks instead of `instanceof` checks on non-Error values - Never use `any` - use `unknown` with type guards when needed ## API Route Patterns - Use Next.js 15 style with `params` as Promise - Use named exports (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) for route handlers - Use `.catch(() => ({}))` for body parsing error handling - Use `NextResponse.json({ error })` response shape - Validate input at boundaries using Zod schemas ## Prisma Patterns - Import Prisma client from `@/lib/db` - Use conditional spread for PATCH updates (e.g., `...(data || {})`) - Use `.catch(() => null)` for not-found handling ## Import Conventions - Use `@/` alias for src imports (e.g., `@/components/Button`) - Import from specific files, avoid barrel exports - Separate client and server imports - never mix them from the same file ## Error Handling - Use tight try/catch around specific I/O operations only - Use `logger.error({ err }, '...')` for structured logging - Import logger from `@kit/shared/logger` - Handle errors gracefully using try/catch and appropriate error types - Use `isRedirectError` from `next/dist/client/components/redirect-error` for client-side redirect handling ## React Patterns - Encapsulate repeated blocks of code into reusable local components - Write small, composable, explicit, well-named components - Always use `react-hook-form` and `@kit/ui/form` for forms - Use client components only when needed for interactivity (use `use client` directive) - Add `data-testid` for E2E tests where appropriate - Prefer server-side data fetching using RSC - Display loading indicators where appropriate ## Next.js Patterns - Use `enhanceAction` for Server Actions from `@kit/next/actions` - Use `use server` in server action files - Export page components using the `withI18n` utility - Add well-written page metadata to pages - Redirect using `redirect` following a server action instead of using client-side router ## Data Fetching Patterns - Use async server components that call loader functions for initial data fetching - Use `enhanceAction` for create/update/delete operations with schema validation - Use `Promise.all()` for parallel data fetching in Server Components ## Linting and Formatting Before committing, always run: ```bash pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck ``` ## Component Organization - Route-specific components: `_components/` directories - Route utilities: `_lib/` for client, `_lib/server/` for server-side - Global components: Root-level directories ## Naming Conventions - Pages: `page.tsx` (Next.js convention) - Loaders: `{feature}-page.loader.ts` - Actions: `{feature}-server-actions.ts` - Schemas: `{feature}.schema.ts` - Components: `kebab-case.tsx`
